task_completion.description: The agent creates the scenario directory and configuration files for the exercise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor showing the creation or editing of files within the exact path '/opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/o) Solent Radar Plotting Exercise/'.

task_completion.description: The agent configures the environment conditions and own ship parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or configuration screen showing own ship 'MV Dorado' set to Lat 50.800, Long -1.150 (or 1.150 W), Heading 270, Speed 12.0. Environment settings should show Time between 01:00-04:00 (or 1.0-4.0), Visibility 2.0-4.0, and Weather <= 1.0.

task_completion.description: The agent configures exactly four traffic vessels with the specified encounter geometries and speeds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Configuration files showing four targets being set up: Target 1 (West, ~14 kts), Target 2 (NW, ~10 kts), Target 3 (East, ~18 kts), and Target 4 (SW, ~6 kts).

task_completion.description: The agent configures the required radar settings for the exercise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Configuration file (e.g., bc5.ini or scenario file) showing ARPA enabled, full radar enabled, range resolution set to 256, and max range set to 48.

task_completion.description: The agent creates and populates the solutions text file with the required maritime calculations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/radar_plotting_solutions.txt' containing entries for all four targets, specifically listing initial range/bearing, CPA, TCPA, target aspect, and required COLREGS actions.

integrity.description: The agent actually creates and edits the configuration and solution files rather than just printing text to the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Use of a text editor (like nano, vim, gedit, or VS Code) to write the scenario and solution files.

integrity.description: The agent performs calculations or uses a tool/simulator to determine CPA and TCPA rather than instantly hallucinating the final values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent writing a script (e.g., Python), using a calculator, or running the Bridge Command simulator to observe the ARPA data before writing the solutions.

integrity.description: The agent uses the exact absolute paths requested for the scenario and solutions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths '/opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/o) Solent Radar Plotting Exercise/' and '/home/ga/Documents/radar_plotting_solutions.txt' are explicitly visible in the editor or terminal commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses Bridge Command, a real maritime simulator. Verified parameters: Own ship must be at 50.800 N, -1.150 W (Solent region) with heading 270 (due West) and speed 12.0 kts. Environment must be night (time 1.0-4.0 in decimal), visibility 2.0-4.0 nm, weather <= 1.5. Radar settings must include ARPA on, full radar on, and max range 48. Exactly 4 traffic vessels must be configured to create COLREGS Rule 13 (overtaking), Rule 14 (head-on), and Rule 15 (crossing) encounters. The solutions file must contain CPA (Closest Point of Approach), TCPA (Time to CPA), target aspect, and COLREGS actions for each vessel.
